calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Mix for (Administrator) Parry Tallmadge.

The Court stated that the issue is trying to figure out why American Express will not take the payment. Ms. Mix reported that they have called American Express and confirmed that there is no debt owed, that American Express is reticent, that they requested a call back but never received one and that it was possibly assigned to Estate Recovery which no longer exists. The Court asked if American Express has either acknowledged or denied the assignment. Ms. Mix stated that they are unable to obtain any information other than American Express has no outstanding claim or debt towards this decedent, that it is up to American Express to provide a change of address, that they believe this has gotten lost in American Express' administration and that it is not fair to make Mr. Tallmadge straighten out American Express. The Court stated that they cannot close the estate with an acknowledged creditor not paid and asked when the last conversation took place. Ms. Mix reported that either her legal assistant or her partner spoke with a representative of American Express in April but they do not have a name, that they had last heard from them in 2010, that there is nothing further Mr. Tallmadge can do and that they understand that the beneficiary may remain liable for the claim. Ms. Mix asked the court to take into consideration that the bond is coming up for renewal and that it is an unnecessary expense to the estate. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-14-2015 Continued To May-19-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Mix for and with (Administrator) Parry Tallmadge.

Ms. Mix reported that they have filed a claim with the state regarding any escheated property, that Mr. Tallmadge recalls the decedent having a safe deposit box at Wells Fargo but are unable to locate one, that they are not positive that a box even existed, that no invoices have been received for a box, all other accounts at Wells Fargo had been closed and that the box could have been abandoned or is with the state. Ms. Mix explained the American Express claim, that it had been settled by former Special Administrator Mr. Lucas, the claim was filed c/o Estate Recoveries, a check was sent but came back, American Express was contacted regarding the claim and stated they have no account for Mr. Lemay, that there is no balance due and that there has been no communication from American Express or Estate Recoveries since 2010. In regards to the Marriott shares Ms. Mix stated that they are waiting to hear from the state regarding the claim that was filed and that Mr. Tallmadge is the beneficiary. The court stated that it would continue the matter for response from the state on the claim and for Ms. Mix to submit a proposal regarding how they intend to handle the American Express approved claim. Ms. Mix asked if it was possible to expunge the bond, that it is excessive. The court responded that there are still some assets until the final distribution is approved and that a request regarding the bond is not before the court now. The accounting petition was continued to May 19, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Sep-17-2014 Continued To Oct-15-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Lachona for Thomas Lucas (former Special Administrator).

Mr. Lachona stated that he is appearing as an interested Party, that there was an order granting fees filed four years ago and that he is concerned about administration of the Estate. Mr. Lachona also stated that he would like to see an accounting, that the inventory and appraisal indicates $300,000.00, and requested a citation be issued. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney Dennis Livingston and the Administrator Parry Talmage to appear on October 15, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why a petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. The order to appear will include an order that bond is to be filed in the amount of $300,000.00 within one week of the order going out. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto CSR# 5229. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: A. Aguerre.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-23-2007 Continued To Aug-29-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Dennis Livingston appearing for and with Parry Tallmadge. Amy Harrington and John St. John appearing for Linda Harrington. Counsel have been unable to come up with a special administrator that they have not represented. They are asking the court to appoint somebody. Court will issue an order appointing special administrator and notify counsel. They are starting discovery. Court is referring this matter to the Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed not later than 8/22. Matter is continued to 8/29 for status conference. If the case is not settled, Court will set a trial date.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Soret-Matley CSR# 9225. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: A. Aguerre.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar May-23-2007 Continued To Aug-29-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-16-2007 Continued To May-23-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Amy Harrington appearing for petitioner Linda Harrington. Dennis Livingston appearing for and with petitioner Parry Tallmadge. Parties are planning to conduct discovery. They will need four month. Court wants parties think about mediation. Counsel can come in to request for referral to pro bono mediation before the next hearing.
The issue on appointment of special administrator argued. Court will appoint a neutral party as the special administrator. Court wants parties to name the special administrator. Matter is continued to 5/23.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Dave Davenport CSR# 1694. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Handa.
